title: The Magic Words
lesson: Kind words like "please" and "thank you" make people smile.

gists: In a cozy home kitchen, the child sits at a wooden table, cheerful, while the grandmother in the background holds a plate of cookies.
In the kitchen, the child reaches eagerly toward the cookies with eyes fixed on the plate while the grandmother holds the plate, watching patiently — the child wants a cookie.
In the kitchen, the child clasps their hands politely as if saying 'please' with a warm smile while the grandmother beams, holding the plate — the child remembers the magic word.
In the kitchen, the child reaches out with both hands to receive a cookie while the grandmother leans in offering the plate — the cookie is given.
In the kitchen, the child holds a cookie close with both hands as if saying 'thank you', joyful, while the grandmother rests a hand on her heart, warm.
In the kitchen in golden light, the child shares the plate of cookies while the grandmother leans close with a hand on their shoulder — cosy, happy, kind words shared.
texts: {NAME} sat at the kitchen table. Grandma had a plate of warm cookies. "Mmm, they smell delicious!" said {NAME}.
{NAME} really, really wanted one and reached a hand toward the plate. "Ooh, cookies..." said {NAME}, eyes wide.
Then {NAME} remembered the magic word. "Please may I have a cookie?" {NAME} asked with a smile. Grandma's eyes twinkled.
"Of course you may!" said Grandma, holding the plate out to {NAME}. "Thank you for asking so nicely."
{NAME} took a cookie and held it close. "Thank you, Grandma!" said {NAME}. Grandma put a hand on her heart, feeling warm and glad.
Together they shared the cookies, cosy and happy. "Kind words are the best," said Grandma. "Please and thank you make everyone smile!" said {NAME}.
